name: Iconic Game Lines
description: The unforgettable lines that gamers quote, from the classics to modern blockbusters.
author: Quips Editorial

quotes.notes: The opening narration written by Tim Cain and voiced by Ron Perlman; it became the signature line of the entire Fallout series.

quotes.content: It's dangerous to go alone! Take this.
quotes.notes: Spoken by the cave Old Man who hands Link his first sword; a landmark of Nintendo's early English localization.

quotes.content: Thank you Mario! But our princess is in another castle!
quotes.notes: A Mushroom Retainer delivers this after each of the first seven castles until the real Bowser is defeated.
Scrawled in hidden dens by the survivor Doug Rattmann as a warning about GLaDOS; it is graffiti, not a line GLaDOS speaks.

quotes.content: A man chooses, a slave obeys.
quotes.notes: Andrew Ryan's line during the climactic confrontation revealing the game's mind-control twist.
The trigger phrase used to control the player throughout BioShock, exposed in Ryan's office scene.
Peppy's advice during combat; the maneuver is technically an aileron roll, not a barrel roll.
Deckard Cain's invitation in Tristram, part of his fuller greeting 'Hello, my friend. Stay awhile and listen.'

quotes.source: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time, Nintendo (1998)
quotes.notes: The fairy companion Navi's repeated attention-grabbing call, famous (and infamous) among players.

quotes.content: Nothing is true, everything is permitted.
quotes.notes: The central maxim of the Assassin order, adapted from the historical Assassins of Alamut, and the franchise's namesake.
The name of Solaire's signature gesture, embraced by the community as the game's rallying cry.
Exact-wording axis fails: the game named the traveler ('[Name] has died of dysentery'); lead designer Philip Bouchard confirms the second-person phrasing is a popular misremembering.

quotes.content: All your base are belong to us.
quotes.notes: The mistranslated villain line from the 1991 Sega Mega Drive port of Toaplan's arcade game; a foundational internet meme.

quotes.content: The right man in the wrong place can make all the difference in the world.
quotes.notes: The G-Man's opening monologue as he wakes Gordon Freeman from stasis.

quotes.content: I used to be an adventurer like you. Then I took an arrow in the knee.
quotes.source: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim, Bethesda Game Studios (2011)
quotes.notes: A procedurally spoken guard idle line written by Emil Pagliarulo that became 2011's breakout gaming meme.
Scorpion's shout as he harpoons opponents with his rope spear; voiced by co-creator Ed Boon.
The announcer's cue prompting the player to perform a Fatality on a defeated opponent.
The Protoss warning when supply is capped; a long-running strategy-game meme.
The escalating Game Over cry from Snake's codec allies, most memorably Colonel Campbell.
Duke's signature catchphrase, voiced by Jon St. John (itself a nod to Army of Darkness).

quotes.content: You were almost a Jill sandwich!
quotes.notes: Barry's line after Jill narrowly escapes a descending-ceiling trap; emblematic of the game's stilted voice acting.

quotes.source: Metal Gear Solid V: The Phantom Pain, Kojima Productions (2015)
quotes.notes: Snake's reveal line and the game's central tagline; the phrase first appeared in Metal Gear Solid 2 (2001).

quotes.source: Deus Ex: Human Revolution, Eidos Montreal (2011)
quotes.notes: Adam Jensen's lament over his forced cybernetic augmentations, heavily featured in the game's marketing.

quotes.content: You're gonna have a bad time.
quotes.notes: Sans's warning to a murderous player before the Snowdin confrontation on a genocide route.

quotes.content: This was a triumph. I'm making a note here: huge success.
quotes.notes: The opening lines of GLaDOS's end-credits song 'Still Alive,' written by Jonathan Coulton and sung by Ellen McLain.

quotes.content: When life gives you lemons, don't make lemonade. Make life take the lemons back!
quotes.notes: The opening of Cave Johnson's 'combustible lemon' rant, one of Portal 2's most-quoted monologues.
The Priest's chant while converting enemy units, sampled from a world-music library and now a series in-joke.
Master Chief's terse reply aboard Cairo Station as the Covenant attack begins.

quotes.source: Pokemon Red and Blue, Game Freak (1998)
quotes.notes: The combat message for a type-advantaged hit; this English phrasing debuted in the 1998 North American release.

quotes.source: Warcraft II: Tides of Darkness, Blizzard Entertainment (1995)
quotes.notes: The Orc Peon's acknowledgment when given an order, voiced by Bill Roper and reused across the Warcraft series.
The Butcher's greeting when the player enters his room, a jump-scare moment burned into players' memories.
